purpose: A query matching no rule's target filters the ruleset to empty, and on this node-heavy graph (2 edges < 3 nodes, so the clause-reorder rebuild never runs) the plain empty list reaches kernel dispatch: reason() raises numba's ValueError('cannot compute fingerprint of empty list') — the raise is the PLAIN-empty-list arm only; an edge-heavy graph reorders the emptied ruleset into a typed list and completes (reason-queries-no-match-edge-heavy). The filtering itself still happened and is permanent: post-raise get_rules returns the empty (plain, no longer numba-typed) list the filter assigned (pyreason.py:1595).
provenance: docs/surface.md type:Query + fn:reason queries-filter (empty-survivor arm); pyreason.py:1594-1595, filter_ruleset.py:29-34 at the pin; screened live 2026-07-07 (screen_queries.py mode=none)
